session.user:
whats a systematic fix to the circular dep in src/erk/core/context.py added in this PR
# 13. Create health check runner (inline import: circular dependency with health_checks)
from erk.core.health_checks.runner import RealHealthCheckRunner
Resolved by Nick Schrock on line 674
Reopen
health_check_runner = RealHealthCheckRunner()
session.assistant: Let me explore the circular dependency structure first.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 905 chars]...ndled in the codebase (e.g., gateway ABCs)
Be thorough - trace every import in the chain.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document.
## Circular Dependency Analysis Report
Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, I've traced the complete circular dependency chain and identified several key findings.
### The Circular Dependency Chain
**Full path of the cycle:**
``` src/erk/core/context.py (line 21-28) ↓ imports real implementations src/erk/core/codex_prompt_executor.py src/erk/core/prompt_executor.py src/erk/core/completion.py src/erk/core/script_writer.py src/erk/core/services/objective_list_service.py src/erk/core/services/plan_list_service.py src/erk/core/shell.py ↓ src/erk/core/health_checks/__init__.py (line 37) ↓ imports at module load time from erk.core.context import ErkContext ↓ IMPORT CONFLICT HERE src/erk/core/health_checks/runner.py (line 16, TYPE_CHECKING guard) ↓ receives ErkContext from __init__.py ↓ CYCLE COMPLETES → context.py can't import health_checks.runner at top level ```
... [125 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Good analysis. Let me verify a few key files to confirm the dependency chain and existing patterns.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Health check implementations for erk doctor command. 2→ 3→This module provides diagnostic checks for erk setup, including 4→CLI availability, repository configuration, and Claude settings. 5→ 6→Submodules: 7→ models - CheckResult dataclass 8→ erk_version - check_erk_version 9→ required_tool_version - check_required_tool_version 10→ claude_cli - check_claude_cli 11→ graphite_cli - check_graphite_cli 12→ github_cli - check_github_cli 13→ github_auth - check_github_auth 14→ workflow_permissions - check_workflow_permissions 15→ erk_queue_pat_secret - check_erk_queue_pat_secret 16→ anthropic_api_secret - check_anthropic_api_secret 17→ uv_version - check_uv_version 18→ hooks_disabled - check_hooks_disabled 19→ statusline_configured - check_statusline_configured 20→ gitignore_entries - check_gitignore_entries 21→ post_plan_implement_ci_hook - check_post_plan_implement_ci_hook 22→ post_init_hook - check_post_init_hook 23→ legacy_prompt_hooks - check_legacy_prompt_hooks 24→ claude_erk_permission - check_claude_erk_permission 25→ plans_repo_labels - check_plans_repo_labels 26→ repository - check_repository 27→ claude_settings - check_claude_settings 28→ user_prompt_hook - check_user_prompt_hook 29→ exit_plan_hook - check_exit_plan_hook 30→ hook_health - check_hook_health
... [126 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Health check runner abstraction for dependency injection. 2→ 3→Provides an ABC for running health checks, enabling tests to inject 4→fake results without monkeypatching. 5→""" 6→ 7→from __future__ import annotations 8→ 9→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 10→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 11→ 12→from erk.core.health_checks import run_all_checks 13→from erk.core.health_checks.models import CheckResult 14→ 15→if TYPE_CHECKING: 16→ from erk_shared.context.context import ErkContext 17→ 18→ 19→class HealthCheckRunner(ABC): 20→ """Abstract interface for running health checks.""" 21→ 22→ @abstractmethod 23→ def run_all(self, ctx: ErkContext, *, check_hooks: bool) -> list[CheckResult]: ... 24→ 25→ 26→class RealHealthCheckRunner(HealthCheckRunner): 27→ """Production implementation that delegates to run_all_checks.""" 28→ 29→ def run_all(self, ctx: ErkContext, *, check_hooks: bool) -> list[CheckResult]: 30→ return run_all_checks(ctx, check_hooks=check_hooks)
... [1 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Unified context for erk and erk-kits operations. 2→ 3→This module provides ErkContext - the unified context that holds all dependencies 4→for erk and erk-kits operations. 5→ 6→The ABCs for erk-specific services (PromptExecutor, ConfigStore, ScriptWriter, 7→PlanListService) are defined in erk_shared.core, enabling 8→proper type hints without circular imports. Real implementations remain in erk. 9→""" 10→ 11→from __future__ import annotations 12→ 13→from dataclasses import dataclass 14→from pathlib import Path 15→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 16→ 17→from erk_shared.context.types import ( 18→ GlobalConfig, 19→ LoadedConfig, 20→ NoRepoSentinel, 21→ RepoContext, 22→) 23→ 24→if TYPE_CHECKING: 25→ from erk.artifacts.paths import ErkPackageInfo 26→ from erk.core.health_checks.runner import HealthCheckRunner 27→ from erk_shared.core.objective_list_service import ObjectiveListService 28→ 29→from erk_shared.core.llm_caller import LlmCaller 30→from erk_shared.core.plan_list_service import PlanListService
... [237 lines omitted] ...
135→ RuntimeError: If not in a git repository 138→ raise RuntimeError("Not in a git repository") 198→ raise RuntimeError("graphite_branch_ops must be set when Graphite is enabled")

660→ real_claude_installation: ClaudeInstallation = RealClaudeInstallation() 661→ real_agent_launcher: AgentLauncher = RealAgentLauncher() 662→ real_agent_docs: AgentDocs = RealAgentDocs() 663→ if dry_run: 664→ real_agent_docs = DryRunAgentDocs(real_agent_docs) 665→ prompt_executor = create_prompt_executor( 666→ global_config=global_config, 667→ console=console, 668→ ) 669→ 670→ # 12. Create package info 671→ package_info = ErkPackageInfo.from_project_dir(cwd) 672→ 673→ # 13. Create health check runner (inline import: circular dependency with health_checks) 674→ from erk.core.health_checks.runner import RealHealthCheckRunner 675→ 676→ health_check_runner = RealHealthCheckRunner() 677→ 678→ # 14. Create context with all values 679→ return ErkContext( 680→ git=git, 681→ github=github, 682→ github_admin=RealGitHubAdmin(), 683→ plan_store=plan_store, 684→ graphite=graphite, 685→ graphite_branch_ops=graphite_branch_ops, 686→ console=console, 687→ shell=RealShell(), 688→ codespace=RealCodespace(), 689→ agent_launcher=real_agent_launcher,

1→"""Application context with dependency injection. 2→ 3→This module provides factory functions for erk CLI context creation. 4→The unified ErkContext dataclass is defined in erk_shared.context and 5→re-exported here for backwards compatibility. 6→""" 7→ 8→from __future__ import annotations 9→ 10→import shutil 11→from collections.abc import MutableMapping 12→from pathlib import Path 13→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast 14→ 15→import click 16→import tomlkit 17→ 18→# Re-export types from erk_shared.context and erk.artifacts.paths 19→from erk.artifacts.paths import ErkPackageInfo as ErkPackageInfo 20→from erk.cli.config import load_config, load_local_config, merge_configs_with_local 21→from erk.core.codex_prompt_executor import CodexPromptExecutor 22→from erk.core.completion import RealCompletion 23→from erk.core.prompt_executor import ClaudePromptExecutor 24→from erk.core.repo_discovery import discover_repo_or_sentinel, ensure_erk_metadata_dir 25→from erk.core.script_writer import RealScriptWriter 26→from erk.core.services.objective_list_service import RealObjectiveListService 27→from erk.core.services.plan_list_service import PlannedPRPlanListService 28→from erk.core.shell import RealShell 29→ 30→# Re-export ErkContext from erk_shared for isinstance() compatibility
... [10 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Now I have the full picture. Let me look at how other service ABCs are structured in erk_shared.
session.tool_result: 1→"""Shell activation script writing abstraction - ABC and types. 2→ 3→This module provides ScriptResult and the abstract ScriptWriter interface. 4→The real implementation (RealScriptWriter) remains in erk.core.script_writer. 5→ 6→ScriptResult is a self-documenting return type for commands that generate shell 7→activation scripts. The key insight is that ScriptResult knows how to output 8→itself correctly to the right stream (stdout vs stderr), preventing an entire 9→class of shell integration bugs. 10→""" 11→ 12→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 13→from dataclasses import dataclass 14→from pathlib import Path 15→ 16→ 17→@dataclass(frozen=True) 18→class ScriptResult: 19→ """Result of writing an activation script. 20→
session.assistant: Clear pattern. The ABC lives in `erk_shared/core/`, the real implementation stays in `erk/core/`. Now let me write the plan.

session.tool_result: File created successfully at: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/functional-zooming-yao.md
session.assistant: Let me verify one thing — whether `CheckResult` has any circular dep issues that would complicate moving the ABC.
session.tool_result: 1→"""Shared data model for health check results.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass 4→ 5→ 6→@dataclass(frozen=True) 7→class CheckResult: 8→ """Result of a single health check. 9→ 10→ Attributes: 11→ name: Name of the check 12→ passed: Whether the check passed 13→ message: Human-readable message describing the result 14→ details: Optional additional details (e.g., version info) 15→ verbose_details: Extended details shown only in verbose mode 16→ warning: If True and passed=True, displays warning instead of success 17→ info: If True and passed=True, displays info (informational, not success) 18→ remediation: Optional command/action to fix a failing check 19→ """ 20→ 21→ name: str 22→ passed: bool 23→ message: str 24→ details: str | None = None 25→ verbose_details: str | None = None 26→ warning: bool = False 27→ info: bool = False 28→ remediation: str | None = None 29→

1→"""Fake health check runner for testing.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING 6→ 7→from erk.core.health_checks.models import CheckResult 8→from erk.core.health_checks.runner import HealthCheckRunner 9→ 10→if TYPE_CHECKING: 11→ from erk_shared.context.context import ErkContext 12→ 13→ 14→class FakeHealthCheckRunner(HealthCheckRunner): 15→ """Test fake that returns pre-configured check results.""" 16→ 17→ def __init__(self, *, results: list[CheckResult]) -> None: 18→ self._results = results 19→ 20→ def run_all(self, ctx: ErkContext, *, check_hooks: bool) -> list[CheckResult]: 21→ return self._results 22→
session.assistant: Good. `CheckResult` in `models.py` is a standalone dataclass with no circular deps. The fake imports the ABC from `runner.py`. Now let me also check if `CheckResult` should move to `erk_shared` or stay — and whether `models.py` is imported by anything in `erk_shared`.

session.assistant:
The plan has two parts:
1. **Guard the import** in `health_checks/__init__.py` — add `from __future__ import annotations` and put the `ErkContext` import behind `TYPE_CHECKING`. This breaks the runtime cycle.
2. **Move the ABC to `erk_shared/core/`** — matches how every other service ABC is structured (ScriptWriter, PromptExecutor, etc.). Then update the 4 files that import `HealthCheckRunner` to use the new path, and promote the inline import in `context.py` to a top-level import.
6 files touched, 1 new file created.

PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/functional-zooming-yao.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Fix HealthCheckRunner Circular Dependency Current context: (wt:erk-slot-04) (br:plnd/fix-doctor-warning-allowli-03-06-1023) (pr:#8847)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:plnd/fix-"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch. Read the plan from: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/functional-zooming-yao.md Implement changes, run CI, and optionally 'erk pr submit' when done.
... [6 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
---
# Fix HealthCheckRunner Circular Dependency
## Context
`src/erk/core/context.py` line 674 has an inline import to work around a circular dependency:
```python # 13. Create health check runner (inline import: circular dependency with health_checks) from erk.core.health_checks.runner import RealHealthCheckRunner ```
**The cycle:** `context.py` imports many `erk.core.*` modules at top level. If it also imported `runner.py` at top level, `runner.py` imports `run_all_checks` from `health_checks/__init__.py` (line 12), which imports `ErkContext` from `erk.core.context` (line 37) — completing the cycle before `context.py` finishes loading.
## Two-part fix
### Part 1: Guard `ErkContext` import in `health_checks/__init__.py`
The `__init__.py` imports `ErkContext` unconditionally (line 37) but only uses it as a type annotation for `run_all_checks()`. Fix:
**File: `src/erk/core/health_checks/__init__.py`** - Add `from __future__ import annotations` at top (makes annotations strings at runtime) - Move `from erk.core.context import ErkContext` behind `if TYPE_CHECKING:` guard - This breaks the runtime cycle: importing `health_checks` no longer triggers importing `context`
### Part 2: Move ABC to `erk_shared` (matches codebase pattern)
Every other service ABC (ScriptWriter, PromptExecutor, PlanListService, LlmCaller) lives in `erk_shared/core/` while the real impl stays in `erk/core/`. HealthCheckRunner should follow suit.
**File: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/health_check_runner.py`** (new) - Move `HealthCheckRunner` ABC here - Import `CheckResult` from `erk.core.health_checks.models` behind `TYPE_CHECKING` (it's only used in annotations) - Use `TYPE_CHECKING` guard for `ErkContext` (same pattern as other ABCs)
**File: `src/erk/core/health_checks/runner.py`** - Remove `HealthCheckRunner` ABC class definition - Import ABC from `erk_shared.core.health_check_runner` - `RealHealthCheckRunner` stays here
**File: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py`** (line 26) - Change `from erk.core.health_checks.runner import HealthCheckRunner` → `from erk_shared.core.health_check_runner import HealthCheckRunner`
**File: `src/erk/core/context.py`** - Line 91: Change TYPE_CHECKING import to `from erk_shared.core.health_check_runner import HealthCheckRunner` - Line 674: Move `from erk.core.health_checks.runner import RealHealthCheckRunner` to top-level imports, remove inline import comment
**File: `tests/fakes/health_check_runner.py`** (line 8) - Change `from erk.core.health_checks.runner import HealthCheckRunner` → `from erk_shared.core.health_check_runner import HealthCheckRunner`
## Verification
- Run `make fast-ci` (unit tests, ty, ruff, prettier) - Confirm no inline import remains in context.py for health checks - Confirm the import chain `context.py` → `runner.py` → `__init__.py` no longer cycles at runtime
---

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Fix HealthCheckRunner Circular Dependency\nCurrent context: (wt:erk-slot-...[truncated 425 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Fix HealthCheckRunner Circular Dependency Current context: (wt:erk-slot-04) (br:plnd/fix-doctor-warning-allowli-03-06-1023) (pr:#8847)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
Launching skill: erk:plan-save
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
**Step 1:** No flags passed — all flags empty.
**Step 2:** Branch slug from title "Fix HealthCheckRunner Circular Dependency": `fix-healthcheck-circular-dep`
**Step 3:** Summary: "Breaks the circular dependency between context.py and health_checks by guarding the ErkContext import in health_checks/__init__.py with TYPE_CHECKING and moving the HealthCheckRunner ABC to erk_shared/core/ to match the existing service ABC pattern."
**Step 4:** Running save command.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" --bra...[truncated 191 chars]... the HealthCheckRunner ABC to erk_shared/core/ to match the existing service ABC pattern."
Save plan as draft PR via erk exec plan-save

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "plan_number": 8852, "plan_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8852", "title": "[erk-plan] Fix HealthCheckRunner Circular Dependency", "branch_name": "plnd/fix-healthcheck-circular-d-03-06-1303", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/.erk/scratch/sessions/fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21/plan_snapshots/000001-22387e0c"}
session.assistant: Plan saved. Now pushing the planning session.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Get session file path for push

PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8852 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement plan #8852: In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement -d

session.assistant:
Plan #8852 saved. Here are your next steps:
**Implement plan #8852:** ``` In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8852 --script)" && erk implement -d ```
**Checkout plan #8852:** ``` In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8852 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8852 ```
**Dispatch plan #8852:** ``` CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8852 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch ```
session.user:
# /erk:pr-dispatch
## Goal
Find the most recent plan created in this conversation and dispatch it for remote AI implementation via `erk pr dispatch`.
## What This Command Does
1. Search conversation for the last plan reference 2. Extract the plan number 3. Run `erk pr dispatch <plan_number>` to trigger remote implementation
## Finding the Plan
Search the conversation from bottom to top for these patterns (in priority order):
1. **Draft PR reference**: `saved as draft PR #<number>` or `draft PR #<number>` 2. **Pull request URL**: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<number>` 3. **Plan URL**: `https://github.com/.../pull/<number>` (legacy: `https://github.com/.../issues/<number>`)
Extract the plan number from the most recent match.
## Execution
Once you have the plan number, run:
```bash erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session:
```bash erk exec marker delete --session-id "fd943c69-0f50-4df5-92a7-00618bf24c21" plan-saved-issue ```
Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state).
## Error Cases
- **No plan found in conversation**: Report "No plan found in conversation. Run /erk:plan-save first to create a plan." - **erk pr dispatch fails**: Display the error output from the command (erk pr dispatch validates the plan)
session.assistant: The most recent plan is #8852. Dispatching it now.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Dispatch plan #8852 for remote AI implementation

Dispatching workflow: plan-implement.yml Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 3/11) ✓ Workflow dispatched. ✓ Dispatch metadata written Posting queued event comment... ✓ Queued event comment posted
✓ 1 plan(s) dispatched successfully!

tool_use.param: Clear plan-saved marker for this session
